quranicRelevance: Disliking a command ('kurhun lakum'), acting under compulsion (Ikrah), and doing things with difficulty/hardship ('kurhan').

keyDerivations.meaning: To dislike, detest, hate, or find objectionable

figurativeOrRareUsages: حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُ كُرْهًا (46:15): His mother bore him with hardship/pain.
كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ القِتَالُ وَهُوَ كُرْهٌ لَكُمْ: Warfare is ordained for you though it is dislikeable/hard.
discardedNoise: Grammatical discussions on the voweling of (كُره) vs (كَرْه).
Obscure details of desert heat causing distress.
summaryEn: The root represents dislike and hardship, outlining natural human aversion to difficulty and the legal bounds of coercion (Ikrah).
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الكراهية والمشقة، موضحاً النفور البشري الطبيعي من الصعوبات والحدود الشرعية للإكراه والضغوط.
rawText: أَكْرَهْتُهُ عَلَى كَدَا 4 I made him to do such a thing against his will. (S.) الكَراَهِيَةُ للشَّىْءِ signifies البُغْضُ لَهُ and عَدَمَ مُلَاءَمَتِهِ. (MF in art. ابى.) تَكَاْرَهَ 6 see 5. تَكَرَّهَهُ 5 He showed, or expressed, dislike, disapprobation, or hatred, of it; see تَاَجَّمَهُ and تَسَخَّطَهُ: and 🡻تَكَارَهَعَليْهِ signifies the same; see قَنَحَ. === تَكَرَّهَ He expressed dislike, displeasure, disapprobation, discontent, or hatred. (IbrD.) See examples in the K, voce أَخّْ, and voce أَفّْ, &c. The above is the prevailing signification, and often occurs. === تَكَرَّهَهُ i. q. كَرِهَهُ, q. v. (K, * TA.) See also تَأَجَّمَهُ. كَارِهٌ Unwilling: see an ex. voce أَسَآءَ. كَرِهَهُ 1 [He disliked, was displeased with, disapproved of, hated, him or it.] الكَرَاهَةُ is the contr. of الإِرَادَةُ and الرِّضَى. (Marg. note in TA.) === كَرِهَهُ (Mgh, Msb), inf. n. كَرَاهَةٌ and كَرَاهِيَةٌ, (Mgh,) or كُرْهٌ and كَرْهٌ, (Msb,) He did not desire it; he disapproved it, or was displeased or discontented with it; (Mgh;) he disliked it; disapproved it; hated it; contr. of حَبَّهُ. (Msb.) === كَرِهَ and 🡻تَكَرَّهَ: see سَخِطَ. كَرِيهٌ Disliked, disapproved of, blamed, or hated; hateful, blameable, displeasing, or odious; as also 🡻مَكْرُوهٌ. كَرَّهْتُ إِلَيْهِ الشَّىْءَ 2 , inf. n. تَكْرِيهُ, I made the thing to be an object of dislike, disapprobation, or hatred, to him. (S, K. *) مَكْرَهٌ A thing that one dislikes, disapproves, or hates, or that one dislikes to do: opposed to مَنْشَطٌ: (TA in art. نشط:) [a thing, or an event, that is an object of dislike or hatred]. مَكْرُوهٌ Foul, abominable, or evil; i. q. سَيِّئٌ; (Beyd, xvii. 40;) and شَرٌّ: (TA:) [held in aversion]. See كَرِيهٌ. === مَكَارِهُ, a pl. of مَكْرُوهٌ. === مَكَارِهُ الدَّهْرِ The afflictions, or calamities, of fortune; syn. نَوَازِلُهُ and شَدَائِدُهُ. (TA.) See also مَكْرَهٌ. مُسْتَكْرَهٌ : see an ex. voce عَرَضٌ.
